title: color-mix()
description: Mixes two colors together in a given color space. Replaces Sass/Less color functions like `lighten()` and `darken()` — you can now do this natively in CSS, including mixing with `transparent`.
html:
Base color
Tint — mix with white
Shade — mix with black
Fade — mix with transparent
challenge: The `.shade` swatch already uses `color-mix()`. Now update `.tint` to use `color-mix(in srgb, #a855f7 50%, white)` to create a lighter purple tint.

alternatives.name: Mix in the oklch color space
alternatives.description: oklch produces more perceptually uniform transitions. Mixing in srgb can produce muddy grey midpoints — oklch keeps colors vibrant and natural-looking across the range.
alternatives.css:
.tint  { background: color-mix(in oklch, #a855f7 50%, white); }
.shade { background: color-mix(in oklch, #a855f7 70%, black); }

realWorldUsage: The most practical production use is palette generation from a single CSS custom property. Define --brand once, then derive every tint, shade, and alpha variant from it: color-mix(in oklch, var(--brand), black 15%) for hover states, color-mix(in oklch, var(--brand), transparent 80%) for subtle backgrounds. Change the one source variable and every derived color updates automatically — no preprocessor required.
